title: Individually assigned server alias address for contacting a server
abstract: To mitigate attacks utilizing compromised DNS caches, a server gateway provides clients with unique IP addresses to contact the server. Packets sent to a server IP address from a particular client which are not linked to that particular with the server gateway are dropped. Thus, even if a client is compromised, the IP address for the server in the client's DNS cache cannot be used by other machines or virtual machines. With a one to one client to server IP address relationship, malicious actors cannot use numerous machines or virtual machines to overload the server with requests.
